150deff5.description: Decompose shape of 5s into non-overlapping 2x2 blocks (colored 8) and 1-wide connecting strips (colored 2)

1b2d62fb.code:
def solve(grid):
    rows = len(grid)
    col_div = None
    for c in range(len(grid[0])):
        if all(grid[r][c] == 1 for r in range(rows)):
            col_div = c
            break
    width = col_div
    result = []
    for r in range(rows):
        row = []
        for c in range(width):
            left_val = grid[r][c]
            right_val = grid[r][col_div + 1 + c]
            row.append(8 if (left_val == 0 and right_val == 0) else 0)
        result.append(row)
    return result
1b2d62fb.description: Grid divided by column of 1s; output 8 where both left and right sides have 0, else 0

1b60fb0c.description: Add 2-colored cells to complete 180-degree rotational symmetry of the 1-colored shape, using the center that requires minimum additions

1bfc4729.description: Two dots split grid into top/bottom zones; each zone gets a rectangle outline with full lines at dot row and nearest grid edge

1caeab9d.description: Move all colored rectangles to align vertically with the 1-colored rectangle, keeping columns fixed

1f0c79e5.description: 2x2 block with 2-colored cells indicating diagonal directions; trail the block diagonally to grid edges

1f642eb9.description: Signal dots project onto nearest edge of the 8-colored rectangular block along their row or column

1f876c06.description: Connect same-colored dot pairs with diagonal lines

1fad071e.description: Count 2x2 blocks of 1s and output that many 1s followed by 0s in a row of 5

2013d3e2.code:
def solve(grid):
    rows = len(grid)
    cols = len(grid[0])
    min_r = min_c = float('inf')
    max_r = max_c = -1
    for r in range(rows):
        for c in range(cols):
            if grid[r][c] != 0:
                min_r = min(min_r, r)
                max_r = max(max_r, r)
                min_c = min(min_c, c)
                max_c = max(max_c, c)
    h = max_r - min_r + 1
    w = max_c - min_c + 1
    half_h = h // 2
    half_w = w // 2
    result = []
    for r in range(min_r, min_r + half_h):
        row = []
        for c in range(min_c, min_c + half_w):
            row.append(grid[r][c])
        result.append(row)
    return result
2013d3e2.description: Extract the top-left quadrant of the symmetric pattern in the grid
